channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Man arrested with weapon 'consistent with' gun in killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO, police say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Police have arrested a 26-year-old man with a weapon consistent with the gun used to kill the head of the largest U.S. health insurer, New York’s police commissioner said Monday.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cynthia Erivo, Demi Moore, Cooper Koch and more react to Golden Globe nominations
item.description: BEVERLY HILLS, Calif. (AP) — The nominations for the Golden Globe Awards are a starry collection of the biggest names from film and television.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ghana's ex-leader is declared the winner of the presidential election
item.description: ACCRA, Ghana (AP) — Ghana’s former leader John Dramani Mahama was officially declared the winner of the presidential election on Monday evening, after voters vented anger at the government’s handling of the economy.
item.dc:creator: FRANCIS KOKUTSE and MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Military pauses Osprey flights again after more metal failures are found in near crash in November
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon is temporarily pausing flights again of its fleet of V-22 Ospreys after weakened metal components possibly played a role in another near crash, the latest setback for an embattled aircraft whose safety problems have grown.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes hit Gaza and also target suspected weapons sites in Syria
item.description: Israeli strikes killed at least six people in the central Gaza Strip, Palestinian medical officials said Monday, while also hitting suspected chemical and long-range weapons sites in Syria to keep them from rebels who seized Damascus. The U.N.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Supreme Court rejects appeal challenging Hawaii gun licensing requirements under Second Amendment
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court rejected a challenge to Hawaii's gun-licensing law on Monday, though three justices expressed a willingness to hear arguments over the issue later. The majority did not explain their reasoning in a brief order declining to take the case.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Zelenskyy open to Western troops providing security for end to war in Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Monday that he's open to the potential deployment of Western troops in Ukraine to guarantee the country’s security as part of a broad effort to end the almost three-year war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Daniel Penny acquitted in NYC subway chokehold case over Jordan Neely's death
item.description: A Marine veteran who used a chokehold on an agitated subway rider was acquitted on Monday in a death that became a prism for differing views about public safety, valor and vigilantism.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Veteran Daniel Penny is acquitted in NYC subway chokehold case over Jordan Neely's death
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Marine veteran who used a chokehold on an agitated subway rider was acquitted on Monday in a death that became a prism for differing views about public safety, valor and vigilantism.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Jersey becomes latest state to prohibit bans on books in school, public libraries
item.description: PRINCETON, N.J. (AP) — New Jersey Democratic Gov. Phil Murphy signed a law Monday to prohibit public and school libraries from banning books in the state and to enshrine protections against civil and criminal charges for librarians who comply with the law.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Gas prices drop to lowest in 3 years, AAA says
item.description: Good news for the millions of drivers on American roads: gas prices are falling. The national average for a gallon of gas has hit a three-year low, the American Automobile Association (AAA) recently announced.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, Fox 5

item.title: Lara Trump stepping down from RNC amid Senate speculation
item.description: Lara Trump is stepping down as co-chair of the Republican National Committee (RNC) as she considers replacing Florida Sen. Marco Rubio (R), whom her father-in-law, President-elect Donald Trump, picked to be secretary of State.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Trump return to DC looms over Kennedy Center Honors
item.description: In a night filled with political moments — including multiple nods to President Joe Biden, a Grateful Dead tribute from former Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Juan Soto agrees to record $765 million, 15-year contract with Mets, AP source says
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Star outfielder Juan Soto and the New York Mets agreed Sunday to a record $765 million, 15-year contract, a person familiar with the deal told The Associated Press, a deal that could escalate to $805 million and is believed to be the largest pact in team sports history.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Netflix must pay $385K to woman exposed as fertility doctor's secret child: court
item.description: A federal court in Indianapolis has ordered Netflix to pay $385,000 to an Indiana woman it outed as the daughter of Donald Cline, a former fertility doctor who secretly used his own sperm to inseminate patients.
item.dc:creator: Austin Hanson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Darnold delivers for Vikings with career-high 347 yards and 5 TDs to beat Falcons, Cousins 42-21
item.description: Darnold added yet another highlight to his brilliant first season with the Vikings, passing for 347 yards and five touchdowns, both career highs, in a 42-21 victory over the foundering Falcons on Sunday to stretch their winning streak to six games.
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tua Tagovailoa's TD pass to Jonnu Smith gives Dolphins 32-26 overtime win over Aaron Rodgers, Jets
item.description: Tagovailoa sealed it with a 10-yard touchdown pass to Jonnu Smith in overtime to help the Dolphins spoil Rodgers' first 300-yard passing game in nearly three years and beat the Jets for the ninth straight time in Miami.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Saints QB Derek Carr injures left hand on dive in 4th quarter of win over Giants
item.description: New Orleans Saints quarterback Derek Carr injured his left hand late in the fourth quarter of Sunday's 14-11 victory over the New York Giants when he went airborne while trying for a first down and crashed to the turf.
item.dc:creator: TOM CANAVAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's 'Meet the Press' interview
item.description: President-elect Trump's first sit-down interview since winning a second White House term aired Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Forever chemicals tainting food supply, destroying American farmers
item.description: Advocates like Erin Brockovich are sounding the alarm. She says when it comes to forever chemicals, “the storm is here.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, ABC 4

item.title: New photos released of suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: The manhunt continues for UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson’s killer as police released new photos of the suspect.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, ABC 4

item.title: Giant statue of the Buddha in New Jersey becomes interfaith hub and spiritual home for many
item.description: FRANKLIN TOWNSHIP, N.J. (AP) — Just off a state highway in New Jersey, one of the largest statues of the Buddha in the United States appears unexpectedly in the middle of a backyard.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's 'Meet the Press' interview
item.description: Trump spoke with "Meet the Press" moderator Kristen Welker, and the two discussed Trump's plans for his first day in office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Syria latest: Syrians celebrate in the streets as Russian media says Assad has arrived in Moscow
item.description: Syrians poured into streets in celebration on Sunday after a stunning rebel advance reached the capital, ending the Assad family’s 50 years of iron rule. Russian state news agencies were reporting that President Bashar Assad and his family had arrived in Moscow and were given asylum.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: First 12-team College Football Playoff set, Oregon seeded No. 1 and SMU edges Alabama for last spot
item.description: The tournament starts Dec. 20-21 with four first-round games involving teams seeded 5-12. It concludes Jan. 20 with the national title game in Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Eddie Pells, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohio bill would require high-demand liquor bottles to be opened when sold
item.description: The bill aims to combat "flippers" who quickly buy up the limited supply of highly sought-after spirits in Ohio to illegally resell them on a secondary market for significantly more than what they paid.
item.dc:creator: Ava Boldizar, WGN 9

item.title: NYPD divers seen searching Central Park lake as manhunt for CEO killer continues
item.description: Divers scoured a lake in New York City's Central Park on Sunday as authorities look for evidence in the slaying of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ousted Syrian leader Assad flees to Moscow, Russian state media say
item.description: Syrians poured into streets echoing with celebratory gunfire on Sunday after a stunning rebel advance reached the capital, putting an end to the Assad family's 50 years of iron rule but raising questions about the future of the country and the wider region.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Notre Dame opens to the public for first time since 2019 fire
item.description: Later on Sunday, an evening Mass will welcome members of the public who secured reservations last week.
item.dc:creator: KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump calls for 'immediate ceasefire' in Ukraine after meeting with Zelenskyy
item.description: In a post on his Truth Social platform, Trump claimed that Moscow and Kyiv have both lost hundreds of thousands of soldiers in a war that “should never have started.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: Doorbell camera captures Illinois garbage truck explosion
item.description: Authorities described the explosion as "significant" and said truck parts were launched into the air, landing several blocks away.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, KRON4

item.title: Disneyland announces closure date for Red Car Trolley attraction
item.description: Disneyland has announced a closing date for the Red Car Trolley attraction at Disney California Adventure as construction for an expansion of Avengers Campus nears.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WGN 9

item.title: MAP: Flu season is here, especially in these states
item.description: Flu activity spiked in states across the U.S. during the last week of November, according to a new report from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Police find bag carried by gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, say he likely fled NYC on bus
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said Friday. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Waymo unveils plan to bring its robotaxi service to Miami
item.description: Waymo is gearing up to bring its robotaxi service to Miami which will accelerate an expansion that's been happening while its hobbled rivals remain in its rearview mirror.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4
